## Summary

Adds Apple, Facebook, and X (Twitter) as OAuth sign-in providers alongside existing Google and Microsoft. Both login and signup pages now offer all 5 providers for an all-inclusive authentication experience.

**Login page order:** Google → Microsoft → Apple → Facebook → X
**Signup page:** Now includes the full OAuth section (previously had no OAuth buttons).

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `app/login/page.tsx` — expanded provider type union, added 3 new OAuth buttons with brand SVG icons
- `app/signup/page.tsx` — added complete OAuth section with all 5 providers + `handleOAuthLogin` function

**Does NOT touch:**
- Supabase auth configuration (providers must be enabled in the Supabase dashboard separately)
- Auth callback handler
- Any backend/pipeline code

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **Supabase provider config required:** The buttons will appear on the UI immediately, but OAuth login will only work for providers that are enabled in the Supabase dashboard (Authentication → Providers). Google and Microsoft (Azure) are already configured. Apple, Facebook, and Twitter need to be enabled with their respective client IDs/secrets in Supabase.
- If a user clicks a provider that isn't configured in Supabase, they'll see a clear error message via the existing error handling — no silent failures.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — UI-only changes to login/signup pages. No evaluation pipeline changes.
